findings.gap: EPIC-12 is the first time we implement Spec Pipeline critique phases (Phase 5 & 7) for agent framework specs. No operational precedent in codebase.

patterns_found.name: EPIC-9 Story Critique Analogy
patterns_found.mechanism: Constitutional gates (Art. I-VII) are validated at hook layer; stories cite gate files; QA validates compliance
patterns_found.applicability: Moderate — gates are simpler than framework spec assessment. Spec Pipeline critique must assess broader architecture coherence, not just gate mechanics.

findings.new_checklist_needed: Yes — QA critique of agent framework spec (Phase 5 checklist) must include: (1) agent persona alignment with commands, (2) workflow determinism verification, (3) authority boundary enforcement, (4) gate coverage analysis, (5) multi-agent interaction completeness
findings.timeline_impact: Phase 5/7 may need 2-3 extra hours to design new critique checklist before executing
findings.evidence: complexity.json: '🔍 Spike may be needed for Spec Pipeline critique patterns (Phase 5/7) — no operational precedent; design new QA gate checklist'

researchTopics.source: EPIC-12-PRD §4 acceptance criteria (FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-3.1, NFR-1.4)

findings.gap: EPIC-12 validation stories (12.1-12.12) must verify all 7 constitutional gates (Art. I-VII) are operational. EPIC-9 implemented the gates themselves; EPIC-12 tests them.

patterns_found.name: Constitutional Gate Architecture (EPIC-9 Completed)
mechanism: Art. I (CLI First): Gate-logger observes all Bash/PowerShell tool use
Art. II (Agent Authority): enforce-agent-authority.cjs blocks non-@devops push/PR
Art. III (Story-Driven): enforce-story-driven.cjs requires active story for commits
Art. IV (No Invention): enforce-no-invention.cjs requires FR-*/NFR-*/CON-* traceability
Art. V-VII (Quality + Boundary): enforce-quality-gates.cjs blocks L1/L2 writes + pre-merge QA
patterns_found.operational_status: ✅ ALL GATES OPERATIONAL — Cont 57 deployed (84 tests PASS, 100% coverage)

findings.testing_strategy: EPIC-12 stories 12.1-12.12 validate each gate by: (1) attempting violation, (2) verifying block, (3) confirming metrics logged, (4) testing override scenarios

findings.gap: EPIC-12-PRD FR-2.1 requires @aiox-master routing to be deterministic. Cont 56 audit measured agent determinism at 87/100 (minor AC clarity gaps on stories 13.3-13.4).

patterns_found.name: Agent Context Loading Pipeline (Cont 60 identified gap: Three-Surface Agent Trap)
patterns_found.source: Cont 56 audit findings; Cont 60 complexity.json flagged
patterns_found.mechanism: Two independent shim systems exist: (1) .claude/agents/aiox-*.md (skill-invocable), (2) .aiox-core/development/agents/*.md (framework templates). These can diverge — causing context inconsistency.
surfaces: PROBLEM: Surface 1 & 2 can contain different versions of agent definition; Surface 3 must reconcile or fail silently
patterns_found.context_determinism_impact: If agent loads from Surface 1 but commands reference Surface 2, context gaps occur. Solution: Unified activation pipeline (unified-activation-pipeline.js) must normalize all three surfaces.
patterns_found.evidence: complexity.json flag: '⚠️ Three-Surface Agent Trap (memory file) — design must account for .aiox-core/development/agents/*.md vs .claude/agents/aiox-*.md divergence (documented in project-epic12-viability.md)'

findings.precedent: Cont 56 State-Truth audit: agent determinism 87/100 (high); agent authority enforcement 100/100 (perfect)
findings.mitigation_in_phase4: Phase 4 (Write Spec) must define how unified-activation-pipeline.js (31KB, touched by 3 stories) orchestrates surface reconciliation. Design must include: (a) priority order (surface 1 > 2 > 3), (b) conflict detection, (c) graceful degradation (missing surface = use fallback), (d) cache strategy for context efficiency

findings.gap: EPIC-12 must demonstrate that all 7 constitutional articles are enforced deterministically across all workflows (SDC, QA Loop, Spec Pipeline, Brownfield).

patterns_found.name: Article II (Agent Authority) — Hardest to Validate
patterns_found.enforcement_mechanism: enforce-agent-authority.cjs: resolves active agent from env vars → inline command scope → hook-metrics.json → blocks non-@devops git push/PR
patterns_found.determinism_risk: Agent detection order (4 env var sources + fallback) — order matters. If env vars are not set, fallback to hook-metrics.json; if that's stale, false negatives occur.
patterns_found.validation_strategy: 12.2 story must test all 4 paths: (1) AIOX_ACTIVE_AGENT set, (2) inline scope set, (3) session field in hook-metrics.json, (4) fallback (all missing) → should default-DENY
patterns_found.precedent: Cont 57 EPIC-9 story 9.2 tested this; 21 tests PASS

patterns_found.name: Article IV (No-Invention) — Multi-Gate System
enforcement_layers: Layer 1: enforce-no-invention.cjs — detects RFC-2119 keywords (MUST/SHALL/...) + reference pattern (FR-*|NFR-*|CON-*|[research:...])
Layer 2: (NEW, story 12.G1) enforce-spec-reference-validation.cjs — validates cited references actually exist in requirements.json
patterns_found.determinism_achieved: Layer 1 + Layer 2 together = zero false positives (cannot cite invented FR-99 if it doesn't exist in requirements.json)
patterns_found.testing_in_epic12: Story 12.4 (Art. IV) must test: (1) valid FR-* cited → allow, (2) invalid FR-* cited → block at Layer 2

findings.multi_article_scenario: Story 12.8 tests compound scenario: @dev tries to commit code to spec.md with invented FR-99, under story 12.4, with non-@devops agent → all 3 gates (Art. III, IV, II) should evaluate; which has priority?
findings.gate_priority_order: Art. II (agent authority) > Art. III (story-driven) > Art. IV (no-invention) > Art. V-VII (quality/boundary) — enforced in gate-logger.cjs decision priority
findings.evidence: Cont 57 metrics: Art. II = 0 violations (perfect); Art. III = 0 violations; Art. IV = 0 violations

researchTopics.source: EPIC-12-PRD §5.5 research-derived testing principles: Ensemble Validation

findings.gap: EPIC-12 requires no single agent to self-certify. Each story (12.1-12.12) is validated by ≥2 independent roles: (1) the agent under test, (2) @qa gate, (3) cross-story integration.

patterns_found.name: Ensemble Validation Pattern (Research-Derived, Cont 43)
patterns_found.mechanism: No agent self-signs off. Validation requires: (a) agent executes task, (b) independent reviewer (@qa) validates outcome, (c) cross-story integration test ensures no regressions
patterns_found.operationally_proven: Cont 57 EPIC-9 validation: each story 9.1-9.5 went through @dev → @qa → @devops handoff; 84 tests aggregate across 5 stories confirm no agent worked in isolation
patterns_found.application_to_epic12: Each story 12.1-12.8 must: (1) be implemented by @dev, (2) reviewed by @qa, (3) aggregated in @qa epic gate (12.11) that checks cross-story coherence

patterns_found.name: Barrier Synchronization (Research-Derived, Cont 43)
patterns_found.mechanism: Explicit phase gates; no story starts in Week N+1 until Week N barrier (entry/exit criteria) is cleared. Prevents partial-completion drift.
patterns_found.in_epic12: Phase gates: Phase 2 (Assess) ✅ → Phase 3 (Research) CURRENT → Phase 4 (Write Spec) → Phase 5 (Critique v1) → Phase 6 (Revise) → Phase 7 (Critique v2) → Phase 8 (Plan). Phase 3 must COMPLETE before Phase 4 starts.

findings.testing_validation: Story 12.G1-12.G3 are gateway stories: G1 (framework modification), G2 (integration test), G3 (cross-story coherence). They happen in parallel with 12.1-12.8 but their completeness gates Phase 8 planning.

keyFindings: ✅ Constitutional gates (Art. I-VII) are fully operational (EPIC-9 completed, 84 tests PASS)
✅ Gate logging & metrics infrastructure is production-ready (gate-logs JSONL, hook-metrics.json)
⚠️ Spec Pipeline critique process (Phase 5/7) is novel; requires new QA checklist design before Phase 4 → Phase 5 transition
⚠️ Three-Surface Agent Trap identified; unified-activation-pipeline.js must define reconciliation strategy in Phase 4 spec
⚠️ Phase 3 Research completion is BLOCKER for Phase 4 (Write Spec); cannot proceed until all 5 research topics are resolved
phase4SpecMustAddress: 1. New QA critique checklist for agent framework specs (Phase 5 gate design)
2. Unified activation pipeline design: surface priorities, conflict resolution, graceful degradation
3. Three-surface agent trap mitigation: how .claude/agents + .aiox-core/development/agents + .claude/skills reconcile
4. Multi-agent validation integration: how stories 12.1-12.8 aggregate for ensemble validation (story 12.11 epic gate)
5. Barrier synchronization enforcement: phase gates in EXECUTION-PLAN.yaml (Phase 8 output)
synthesisAndRecommendations.confidenceReasoning: All research topics have strong precedent (EPIC-9 gates confirmed, Cont 56 audit baseline, research methodology validated in Cont 43). One uncertainty: novel Spec Pipeline critique patterns have no production precedent yet; confidence drops to 85 pending Phase 4 spec review.

nextPhase.deliverable: spec.md (with implementation strategy addressing all 5 research topics)
nextPhase.gateCondition: Research phase complete with confidence >= 80%; Phase 4 can proceed
